headline: The Klue token theft keeps cascading through its customers, now dragging HackerOne, Recorded Future, Snyk, Tanium, and other security vendors into the breach.

emerging_trends.text: INTERPOL's new Asia-Pacific assessment, fresh World Cup ticket cons, and Israeli deepfake face-swap thefts together show generative AI sharpening fraud faster than victims can spot it.
emerging_trends.topic_title: AI Supercharges Phishing and Identity Fraud

emerging_trends.text: The Klue OAuth token theft keeps climbing the supply chain, now reaching HackerOne, Recorded Future, Snyk, and Tanium and proving security vendors face the same cascade as their customers.
emerging_trends.topic_title: Klue Breach Spreads to Security Vendors

emerging_trends.text: From 23 ClawHub plugins squatting official publisher scopes to 282 iOS apps spilling LLM keys, the rush to ship AI features keeps leaving credentials and registries wide open.
emerging_trends.topic_title: AI Plugins and Apps Leak Credentials

topics.title: AI Supercharges Phishing and Identity Fraud
topics.latest_developments: INTERPOL's 2025/2026 Asia and South Pacific assessment names phishing the region's most widespread threat, Israeli investigators tie AI-generated face-swap videos to identity theft, and Wired details cloned-site World Cup scams.
topics.summary: Generative AI now powers convincing phishing, fake ticket sites, and deepfake impersonation across fraud campaigns worldwide; users should verify sellers and account alerts through official channels before paying or clicking.

sources.title: INTERPOL Warns Phishing, Ransomware, and AI Scams Are Rising Across Asia-Pacific
World Cup Scams Are Getting Harder to Spot
He Used AI Agents To Wear Other People's Faces And Steal Money

topics.title: AI Plugins and Apps Leak Credentials
topics.latest_developments: Manifold Security's Ax Sharma found 23 code-executing plugins squatting ClawHub's official @openclaw and @clawhub scopes, while Wake Forest University researchers found 282 of 444 AI-powered iOS apps exposing exploitable LLM credentials or backend access.
topics.summary: AI agent registries and mobile apps ship with weak controls over who publishes packages and how API keys travel, letting attackers run code or steal model-provider credentials; developers should reserve official scopes and proxy keys server-side.

sources.title: 23 ClawHub plugins squatting official scopes expose AI registry security gaps
Hundreds of AI-powered iOS apps found exposing credentials

topics.title: AryStinger Router Botnet
topics.latest_developments: QiAnXin's XLab attributed the botnet and counts at least 4,300 infected routers and rising, framing AryStinger as a pre-intrusion reconnaissance and proxy network rather than the usual DDoS tool.
topics.summary: AryStinger compromises outdated home routers, many of them D-Link models, and turns them into proxies that scout targets before a break-in; owners of legacy routers should patch firmware or replace unsupported hardware.

sources.title: AryStinger Malware Infects 4,300 Legacy Routers to Build Reconnaissance Proxy Network
AryStinger botnet infected thousands of D-Link routers worldwide

topics.title: Klue Breach Spreads to Security Vendors
topics.latest_developments: HackerOne, Huntress, Jamf, OneTrust, Snyk, and Tanium disclosed Klue exposure, widening a confirmed victim roster that already named Recorded Future.
topics.summary: Attackers stole OAuth tokens from sales-intelligence vendor Klue and used them to reach customers' connected Salesforce environments; affected companies should revoke the tokens and audit Salesforce access.

sources.title: More Cybersecurity Firms Disclose Impact From Klue Hack

topics.title: Fortinet Responds to FortiBleed Campaign
topics.latest_developments: Fortinet published its own response to FortiBleed, confirming the campaign assembled a database of more than 86,000 verified working credentials.
topics.summary: FortiBleed harvested login credentials from tens of thousands of internet-facing FortiGate firewalls and VPN gateways; operators should rotate credentials and harden exposed devices.

sources.title: Fortinet Responds to FortiBleed Campaign

topics.title: Canada's CSIS Cleans Botnet-Infected Devices
topics.latest_developments: The Federal Court released a public version of its ruling on June 15 showing the Canadian Security Intelligence Service used threat reduction warrant powers for the first time to reach into infected servers, home routers, and IoT gear on Canadian soil and neutralize two foreign-run botnets.
topics.summary: A Canadian judge let CSIS alter compromised devices inside the country to dismantle botnets run from abroad, the agency's first use of that authority for active cleanup.

sources.title: Canada's Spy Agency Used First-of-Its-Kind Warrant to Clean Botnet-Infected Devices

weather: Today: Showers And Thunderstorms then Showers And Thunderstorms Likely, high 78F.
Tonight: Showers And Thunderstorms then Mostly Cloudy, low 59F.

games.recap: Gonzalez and Reynolds homer as the Pirates hold off the Rockies 8-6

business_politics.title: Starmer Resigns as U.K. Prime Minister
business_politics.latest_developments: Keir Starmer made it official Monday, June 22, announcing he will leave Downing Street within weeks and clearing the path for Andy Burnham to become Britain's seventh prime minister in a decade.
business_politics.summary: Keir Starmer, who led Labour to a landslide in 2024, quit after a backbench rebellion, pushing Britain into its sixth premier in seven years; U.K. gilt yields edged higher and sterling weakened on fiscal uncertainty.

sources.title: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer Quits Amid Rebellion
Keir Starmer steps down as UK prime minister
Gilt Yields Edge Higher After Starmer Resigns

business_politics.title: China Restricts Rare-Earth Trade With U.S. Firms
business_politics.latest_developments: Beijing imposed fresh export controls Monday on dozens of U.S. companies, including rare-earth firms, retaliating after Washington expanded its list of military-linked Chinese companies.
business_politics.summary: China curbed trading and rare-earth exports to dozens of American firms, escalating the tit-for-tat with Washington over critical-minerals supply that feeds defense and electronics manufacturing.

sources.title: China restricts trading with some US rare earth companies
China Slaps Restrictions on Dozens of U.S. Companies

business.title: Buyer Surfaces for Century III Mall
business.latest_developments: Steve Panko, a Chicago developer who founded Brightside CRE, said he wants to buy the shuttered Century III Mall site and will open redevelopment talks with West Mifflin borough soon.
business.summary: Century III Mall in West Mifflin, dead for years, may finally get redeveloped; Brightside CRE founder Steve Panko told PublicSource a plan could take shape in the 'imminent future.'

sources.title: 'Phenomenal' plan for Century III Mall site could emerge soon

business.title: Gecko Robotics Pitches AI Against Labor Shortage
business.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette profiled Pittsburgh's Gecko Robotics, which builds robots and artificial intelligence to inspect industrial infrastructure as a generation of skilled tradespeople nears retirement.
business.summary: Gecko Robotics, a Pittsburgh company, makes wall-climbing robots and AI software to inspect power plants, pipelines, and ships, framing the technology as a fix for the skilled-labor gap left by retiring workers.

sources.title: Can robots and artificial intelligence solve the issue of a skilled generation nearing retirement?

around_town.title: County Council Weighs Bigger Budget for Itself
around_town.latest_developments: A year after raising property taxes 36%, Allegheny County Council members are weighing a proposal to expand their own benefits and lift the cap on the council budget.
around_town.summary: Allegheny County Council, which approved a 36% property-tax increase last year, could soon grant members more benefits and a larger operating budget, the Post-Gazette reported.

sources.title: A year after raising taxes 36%, County Council members could soon give themselves more benefits and a bigger budget

around_town.title: State Elder-Abuse Probe Kept Secret
around_town.latest_developments: Records show Pennsylvania's elder-abuse watchdog ran an investigation that stayed secret while Governor Josh Shapiro's office claimed confidentiality to withhold the files.
around_town.summary: A Pennsylvania state watchdog kept an elder-abuse investigation under wraps, and Governor Josh Shapiro's office cited confidentiality in refusing to release records, the Post-Gazette found.

sources.title: Records show Pennsylvania watchdog's elder abuse probe kept secret as Shapiro's office claims confidentiality

around_town.title: Strong El Nino May Be Forming
around_town.latest_developments: Forecasters say a 'historically strong' El Nino could develop, and the Post-Gazette laid out what the pattern would mean for Pittsburgh's coming seasons.
around_town.summary: A potentially historic El Nino could shape temperature and precipitation across western Pennsylvania in the months ahead, the Post-Gazette reported.

sources.title: A 'historically strong' El Nino could be on the way. What does that mean for Pittsburgh?

events.title: Pittsburgh Symphony's Summer of Movie Music
events.latest_developments: The Pittsburgh Symphony Orchestra is building its summer season around film scores, the Post-Gazette reported.
events.summary: The Pittsburgh Symphony Orchestra devotes its summer programming to movie music, performing scores from the screen.

sources.title: The Pittsburgh Symphony's summer of movie music

around_teams.title: Pirates' Catching Plan After the Bart Trade
around_teams.latest_developments: A Post-Gazette mailbag laid out the Pirates' catching plan now that Joey Bart is gone, pointing to Henry Davis, and weighed whether Marcell Ozuna returns.
around_teams.summary: With catcher Joey Bart dealt to Atlanta, the Post-Gazette's mailbag identified Henry Davis as central to the Pirates' plan behind the plate and questioned Marcell Ozuna's future with the club.

sources.title: Pirates mailbag: What's the plan at catcher after the Joey Bart trade? Is Marcell Ozuna back?

around_teams.title: Brandon Lowe on Fatherhood
around_teams.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette profiled Pirates infielder Brandon Lowe, who described his battle with infertility and IVF as he marked Father's Day.
around_teams.summary: Pirates second baseman Brandon Lowe told the Post-Gazette that fatherhood, reached after infertility and IVF treatment, is 'the greatest gift.'

sources.title: After a battle with infertility, the Pirates' Brandon Lowe sees fatherhood as 'the greatest gift'

team_usa.title: USMNT Faces Rotation Choice vs. Türkiye
team_usa.latest_developments: Having clinched a place in the round of 32, the U.S. men's national team must decide whether to rotate or keep its same starting XI for the final Group D match against Türkiye.
team_usa.summary: The U.S. men's national team, already through to the World Cup knockouts after topping Group D as a co-host, weighs resting starters against staying consistent in its group finale versus Türkiye.

sources.title: USMNT's World Cup success brings dilemma: Rotate the squad

team_usa.title: Alex Freeman's NFL Bloodlines
team_usa.latest_developments: ESPN profiled USMNT defender Alex Freeman, son of former Green Bay Packers Pro Bowl receiver Antonio Freeman, on reaching his first World Cup.
team_usa.summary: Alex Freeman, the U.S. right back and son of Packers Pro Bowl receiver Antonio Freeman, leaned on his father's professional-sports experience to chase his World Cup dream, ESPN reported.

sources.title: How Freeman's Pro Bowl dad helped him live his World Cup dream

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: Dear AI Companies: Stop the "Doom Trolling"
reading.summary: Newport argues that AI companies undercut their own credibility by issuing alarmist warnings about the dangers of their products, a tactic he likens to a carmaker fretting publicly over its bestselling truck.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: Apple Price Increases, Apple Intelligence and the E.U.
reading.summary: Ben Thompson reads Apple's first real price increases alongside its decision to withhold Apple Intelligence Siri features from the European Union, tying both to regulatory and competitive pressure.

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Premium: The Silicon Valley Bubble (Part 2)
reading.summary: Zitron extends his case that Silicon Valley sits atop a bubble, building on his reporting that OpenAI spent $34 billion to generate roughly $13 billion in revenue.

sports_media: Not Just Football with Cam Heyward
